title: Orchestrator CLI

story.i_want_to: verify stories, reject stories with reasons, list pending verifications, and save/load orchestrator state via the CLI
story.so_that: I can perform my orchestration duties — verifying deliverables, rejecting inadequate work, and checkpointing my state for crash recovery — through a simple command interface

rationale: The orchestrator is the trust layer in loopctl's two-tier model. It needs CLI commands to record verification results (pass/fail/partial), reject stories with actionable reasons that auto-reset agent_status, query for stories awaiting verification, and persist its own state for session recovery. These commands are the orchestrator's primary interface for writing to the verified_status track and maintaining crash resilience.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl verify STORY_NUM --result RESULT --summary TEXT' resolves the story number and calls POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with the result (pass, fail, or partial), summary text, and optional findings

'loopctl reject STORY_NUM --reason TEXT' resolves the story number and calls POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with the rejection reason, displaying the updated story with verified_status=rejected and agent_status auto-reset to pending
'loopctl pending --project PROJECT' queries for stories with agent_status=reported_done and verified_status=unverified, displaying the list of stories awaiting orchestrator verification
'loopctl state save --project PROJECT --data JSON' calls PUT /api/v1/orchestrator/state/:project_id with the state data, supporting optimistic locking via the version field
'loopctl state load --project PROJECT' calls GET /api/v1/orchestrator/state/:project_id and displays the stored state data and current version number
The verify command validates that --result is one of: pass, fail, partial; rejects invalid values with a clear error before making the API call
The reject command requires a non-empty --reason flag; fails with an error if reason is missing or empty
The state save command accepts --data as either inline JSON or a file path prefixed with @ (e.g., --data @state.json)
All orchestrator commands respect the --format flag (json, human, csv)
The verify command optionally accepts --findings JSON for structured findings data to include in the verification result

test_cases.name: Verify story sends correct payload
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and orchestrator-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with {result: 'pass', summary: 'All tests passing'} and returns 200
steps: Call Loopctl.CLI.VerifyCommand.run(['2.1', '--result', 'pass', '--summary', 'All tests passing', '--project', project_id], config_path: tmp_path)
expected_results: POST request is sent with result='pass' and summary text
Output shows story with verified_status='verified'

test_cases.name: Reject story sends reason and shows auto-reset
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and orchestrator-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with {reason: 'Missing migration file'} and returns 200 with story showing verified_status=rejected, agent_status=pending
steps: Call Loopctl.CLI.RejectCommand.run(['2.1', '--reason', 'Missing migration file', '--project', project_id], config_path: tmp_path)
expected_results: POST request is sent with the rejection reason
Output shows story with verified_status='rejected'
Output shows agent_status='pending' (auto-reset)

test_cases.name: Pending command lists stories awaiting verification
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and orchestrator-role api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for stories query filtered by agent_status=reported_done, verified_status=unverified, with 3 stories
expected_results: Output lists 3 stories awaiting verification
Each story shows number, title, agent that reported it, and reported_done_at timestamp
JSON format returns array of story objects

test_cases.name: State save sends state data with version
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and orchestrator-role api_key
Req.Test mock expects PUT /api/v1/orchestrator/state/:project_id with {state_key: 'main', state_data: {...}, version: 1} and returns 200
expected_results: PUT request is sent with state_data containing the parsed JSON
Output confirms state was saved with the new version number

test_cases.name: State load retrieves stored state
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and orchestrator-role api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/orchestrator/state/:project_id with {state_key: 'main', state_data: {current_phase: 'p1'}, version: 3}
expected_results: Output includes the state_data JSON
Output includes the version number (3)
JSON format returns the full state object for agent consumption

test_cases.name: Verify rejects invalid result value
steps: Call Loopctl.CLI.VerifyCommand.run(['2.1', '--result', 'maybe', '--summary', 'not sure', '--project', project_id], config_path: tmp_path)
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} indicating 'maybe' is not a valid result
Error message lists valid values: pass, fail, partial
No HTTP request is made

test_cases.name: Reject requires non-empty reason
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} indicating --reason is required
No HTTP request is made

test_cases.name: State save reads data from file with @ prefix
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and orchestrator-role api_key
Temporary file containing valid state JSON
Req.Test mock expects PUT /api/v1/orchestrator/state/:project_id and returns 200
steps: Call Loopctl.CLI.StateCommand.run(['save', '--project', project_id, '--data', '@' <> tmp_state_path], config_path: tmp_path)
expected_results: State JSON is read from the file
PUT request includes the file contents as state_data
Output confirms state was saved

technical_notes: The verify and reject commands share the story number resolution logic from US-14.6 — reuse the shared helper
The pending command may need to make a filtered query to the stories list endpoint with query parameters for agent_status and verified_status, or use a dedicated endpoint if one exists
State save should include optimistic locking: first load the current version, then save with version+1. If the server returns a version conflict (409), inform the user
The --data @file syntax should work identically to the --artifact @file syntax from US-14.6 — extract the file-reading logic into a shared utility
The verify --findings flag should accept the same inline JSON or @file syntax
Consider adding a --state-key flag to state save/load (default: 'main') for orchestrators that maintain multiple state namespaces
